data.title: China’s iQIYI launches $300M convertible bond offering, shares dip
data.description:
iQIYI (NASDAQ:IQ) has launched an offering of US$300M aggregate principal amount of its convertible senior notes due 2030.

The notes will mature on March 15, 2030, unless repurchased, redeemed or converted in accordance with their terms prior to such date and will be senior, unsecured obligations of the company.

The Chinese video streaming platform will use the net proceeds from the sale for the repayment and/or repurchase of existing debt securities and general corporate purposes.

Shares of iQIYI (NASDAQ:IQ) were down 7.85% premarket.

data.title: Stock index futures slide after S&P 500 notches another record close
data.description:
Stock index futures slid on Thursday, a day after the S&P 500 closed at another record high, while investors geared up for more earnings reports.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.53%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.27%.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan saud.

"Markets put in a divergent performance over the last 24 hours, with U.S. assets continuing to reach fresh highs, even as the rest of the world struggled on the back of President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and concerns about Ukraine," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

On the earnings front, companies like Alibaba (BABA) and Walmart (WMT) are expected to report today.

The initial jobless claims will report before the opening bell today, and is expected to rise to 215K.

The February Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index will also land today and is anticipated to come in at 19.4.

The January leading index, which is being released during market hours, is expected to come in at -0.1% M/M.

data.title: Lumber stocks in focus as Trump mulls 25% tariffs in early April; gold notches another record high
data.description:
Lumber stocks are expected to remain in focus after U.S. President Donald Trump said he will announce fresh tariffs over the next month or sooner, adding lumber and forest products to previously announced plans to impose duties on imported cars, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals.

Lumber futures (LB1:COM) were trading -0.91% lower to $612.54 by 6 am ET on Thursday, having gained about 11% YTD.

"I'm going to be announcing tariffs on cars and semiconductors and chips and pharmaceuticals, drugs and pharmaceuticals and lumber, probably, and some other things over the next month or sooner," Trump said at a conference in Miami.

Lumber futures have recently surged past $610 per thousand board feet, reaching a near three-month high as market conditions tighten.

A combination of factors including mill closures, reduced North American production capacity, and tariff concerns have reportedly resulted in increased volatility in the lumber market.

Speaking to reporters on Air Force One, Trump said he was thinking about a 25% tariff on lumber and forest products, to take effect around April 2, when a tariff on autos of around the same amount is expected.

The tariff hikes would put pressure on an already struggling lumber market, which has been dealing with ongoing reductions in North American production capacity, and rising costs, resulting in supply concerns.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XHB), (NASDAQ:WOOD), (NYSEARCA:CUT), (NYSEARCA:NAIL)

Potentially relevant tickers include (WFG), (WY), (LPX), (PCH), (RFP), (OTCPK:CFPZF), (OTCPK:IFSPF), (OTCPK:WFSTF)

Elsewhere, spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) rose to another record high on Thursday when prices crossed $2,954.70 an ounce.

The safe-haven metal has hit successive records this year, after climbing by more than a quarter in 2024, as Trump’s disruptive trade decisions fan demand for the precious metal.

However, CME Group said, despite the supportive conditions, there are "some potential clouds gathering on the horizon for gold. Gold dropped to a near two-month low following the U.S. election."

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Repsol, S.A. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Repsol, S.A. press release (OTCQX:REPYY): Q4 Adjusted income amounted to €3.327 billion, 34% lower than in 2023.
  • Repsol was, thus, able to generate enough cash to improve the remuneration of its shareholders and maintain an investment effort aligned with the 2024-2027 strategic update, with net investments of €5.7 billion during the year.
  • In addition, in the last quarter it reduced its debt by €524 million compared to the end of September. Liquidity stood at €9.453 billion at the end of the year (including undrawn committed credit lines), which represents 3.51 times the maturities of short-term gross debt.
  • Repsol paid a dividend of €0.90 gross per share, approximately 30% higher than in 2023, and it reduced capital by redeeming 60 million treasury shares.

data.title: NYC congestion pricing: Trump pulls approval, state leaders vow to fight back
data.description:
The Trump administration has terminated the approval for New York City's congestion pricing program, which charged drivers entering Manhattan below 60th Street and was aimed at easing traffic and raising funds for upgrading the city's public transit.

"New York State's congestion pricing plan is a slap in the face to working class Americans and small business owners," said transportation secretary Sean Duffy. "Every American should be able to access NYC regardless of their economic means. It shouldn't be reserved for an elite few."

Duffy said the Department of Transportation's approval for the program was being pulled because it provides no toll-free option and the toll rate was set primarily to raise revenue for transit, rather than at an amount needed to reduce congestion.

Ending the program was one of President Donald Trump's campaign promises. "CONGESTION PRICING IS DEAD. Manhattan, and all of New York, is SAVED. LONG LIVE THE KING!" Trump declared.

The Metropolitan Transportation Authority, which is responsible for NYC's public transit, immediately filed a lawsuit to block the DOT's move. MTA CEO Janno Lieber said the program has already "dramatically reduced congestion."

New York Governor Kathy Hochul agreed, and noted that the program has led to faster commute times, a spike in foot traffic to local businesses and increased transit ridership.

"We are a nation of laws, not ruled by a king," she said, clapping back at Trump's statement. "The MTA has initiated legal proceedings to preserve this critical program. We'll see you in court."

data.title: Alibaba beats Q3 estimates
data.description:
• Alibaba press release (NYSE:BABA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.93 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $38.38B (+24.9% Y/Y) beats by $130M.
  • For the quarter ended December 31, 2024, revenue from Cloud Intelligence Group was RMB31,742 million (US$4,349 million), an increase of 13% year-over-year.
  • Income from operations was RMB41,205 million (US$5,645 million), an increase of 83% year-over-year, primarily due to the decrease in impairment of intangible assets as well as the increase in adjusted EBITA.
  • Diluted earnings per ADS was RMB20.39 (US$2.79). Diluted earnings per share was RMB2.55 (US$0.35 or HK$2.75). Non-GAAP diluted earnings per ADS was RMB21.39 (US$2.93), an increase of 13% year-over-year. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share was RMB2.67 (US$0.37 or HK$2.88), an increase of 13% year-over-year.
